reasoning: Munkres's definition of "regular" (§31) explicitly includes the T1 axiom ("Suppose that one-point sets are closed in X. Then X is said to be regular if..."), whereas Mathlib's `RegularSpace` does not include T1. Adding `[T1Space X]` alongside `[RegularSpace X]` recovers exactly Munkres's notion of "regular" — this is surfacing an implicit premise, not strengthening. Without T1, the claim is literally false (e.g., the indiscrete two-point space is a `RegularSpace` but any two distinct points have closure X, so their closures cannot be disjoint). Similarly, "every pair of points" naturally means distinct points (otherwise the conclusion is vacuously false when x = y), so `x ≠ y` is also an implicit premise made explicit. In Munkres, a "neighborhood" of a point is an open set containing it, matching `IsOpen U ∧ x ∈ U`. The conclusion `closure U ∩ closure V = ∅` is a direct translation of "closures are disjoint". The Lean statement faithfully captures the intended mathematical content.
